topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Dramatic Impact, Movement and Entertainment
subject: music

items.q: What does the time signature 6/8 indicate?
items.correct: Two beats per bar, each beat divided into three (compound duple).
items.misconception: Six beats per bar, all equal in stress.
items.why: Students count 6/8 as six equal beats. In practice it's felt as TWO dotted-crotchet beats subdivided into three quavers each - compound duple, not simple sextuple.

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.q: In what year was the world music label adopted by indie labels?
items.why: Correct answer: 1987. “1972” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many areas of inquiry frame IBDP Music since 2020?
items.why: The real figure is 4 areas. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
